steps.title: Identify the two clauses
steps.content: The words before the blank form one independent clause about how the spider does not appear to use camouflage. The words after the blank form another independent clause about the spider waiting visibly in its web. Those two clauses need a proper boundary.

steps.title: Place the contrast marker
steps.content: A creates a comma splice, and B fuses the two clauses without enough punctuation. C puts the comma before rather and the semicolon after it, which does not correctly attach the contrast marker to the second clause. A semicolon before rather plus a comma after it is required. The answer is D.
